log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable tagged as a 'spreader' with an overlay. It's 215KB in size. The tags suggest it's designed to spread and may use domain generation or evasion techniques. I've already s

The IOC summary confirms 2 unique IPs, 3 domains, 1 dropped file, and 1 process created. No registry persistence keys were found. I need to investigate the network activity in detail to understand the

The network activity shows the malware queries 'ipv4bot.whatismyipaddress.com' to resolve an IP (66.171.248.178), which it then connects to via HTTP. It also queries 'politiaromana.bit' via nslookup b

The dropped file 'rkakxa.exe' is the payload. I need to investigate the process activity to understand how this file was executed, what processes were spawned, and if there are any mutexes or other ar

The process activity shows only 'nslookup politiaromana.bit ns1.virmach.ru' was created, but the artifacts section earlier mentioned 'rkakxa.exe' was dropped and written to the Application Data folder

Registry changes confirm no persistence mechanisms were used. The malware appears to be a simple dropper/spreader that: 1) Drops 'rkakxa.exe' to the user's Application Data folder, 2) Queries 'politia
